# Node.js Design Patterns, Fourth Edition (Luciano Mammino & Mario Casciaro)

Before writing, modifying, or planning any code, you MUST use your `read_file` tool to load the relevant guidelines from `.roo/vendor/`. You may read multiple files if the task crosses domains.

## Table of Contents:
    - [The Node.js Platform](.roo/vendor/node/node-js-design-patterns-fourth-edition-luciano-mammino-mario-casciaro/01-the-node-js-platform.md): Designing, architecting, or writing Node.js applications, modules, or services, including I/O performance optimization, module structuring, asynchronous event handling, TypeScript setup, and dependency management.
    - [The Module System](.roo/vendor/node/node-js-design-patterns-fourth-edition-luciano-mammino-mario-casciaro/02-the-module-system.md): Node.js module creation, application structuring, import/export management, interoperability between ECMAScript Modules (ESM) and CommonJS (CJS), and TypeScript module configuration for Node.js environments.
    - [Callbacks and Events](.roo/vendor/node/node-js-design-patterns-fourth-edition-luciano-mammino-mario-casciaro/03-callbacks-and-events.md): Writing, reviewing, refactoring, or architecting asynchronous JavaScript/Node.js code, specifically involving control flow, API design, the Observer pattern (EventEmitter), callback-based interfaces (Continuation-Passing Style), and debugging asynchronous memory leaks and race conditions.
    - [Asynchronous Control Flow Patterns with Callbacks](.roo/vendor/node/node-js-design-patterns-fourth-edition-luciano-mammino-mario-casciaro/04-asynchronous-control-flow-patterns-with-callbacks.md): Node.js code that relies on Callback-based Asynchronous Control Flow, specifically focusing on managing multiple asynchronous operations, collections, nested callbacks, task queues, and race conditions.
    - [Asynchronous Control Flow Patterns with Promises and Async/Await](.roo/vendor/node/node-js-design-patterns-fourth-edition-luciano-mammino-mario-casciaro/05-asynchronous-control-flow-patterns-with-promises-and-async-await.md): Asynchronous JavaScript or Node.js code, specifically involving writing, refactoring, reviewing, or debugging asynchronous paradigms such as Promises, async/await, and managing asynchronous control flows and error handling.
    - [Coding with Streams](.roo/vendor/node/node-js-design-patterns-fourth-edition-luciano-mammino-mario-casciaro/06-coding-with-streams.md): This rule file applies to any task, file, or user request involving Node.js streams, including data ingestion, file I/O, network communication, large dataset processing, data transformation pipelines, backpressure management, and stream composition/piping.
    - [Creational Design Patterns](.roo/vendor/node/node-js-design-patterns-fourth-edition-luciano-mammino-mario-casciaro/07-creational-design-patterns.md): The creation of object instantiation logic, complex object construction, module wiring, dependency management, and structural application design in JavaScript or Node.js, specifically governing the implementation of Creational Design Patterns including Factory, Builder, Revealing Constructor, Singleton, and Dependency Injection.
    - [Structural Design Patterns](.roo/vendor/node/node-js-design-patterns-fourth-edition-luciano-mammino-mario-casciaro/08-structural-design-patterns.md): Architecting, implementing, or refactoring object structures, component relationships, API bridges, or behavioral augmentations in Node.js, specifically focusing on patterns like Proxy (access control), Decorator (dynamic functionality), Adapter (interface bridging), and Change Observer (state observation).
    - [Behavioral Design Patterns](.roo/vendor/node/node-js-design-patterns-fourth-edition-luciano-mammino-mario-casciaro/09-behavioral-design-patterns.md): Designing, refactoring, or implementing object behaviors, algorithmic variations, state-driven logic, data iteration, processing pipelines, or deferred execution tasks in JavaScript, TypeScript, or Node.js, specifically focusing on Behavioral Design Patterns such as Strategy, State, Template, Iterator (including Generators and Async Iterators), Middleware, or Command.
    - [Testing: Patterns and Best Practices](.roo/vendor/node/node-js-design-patterns-fourth-edition-luciano-mammino-mario-casciaro/10-testing-patterns-and-best-practices.md): Triggered when the user requests the creation, modification, or review of test files (e.g., `*.test.js`, `*.test.ts`, `*-test.js`), test configurations, Playwright end-to-end (E2E) scripts, integration tests, or tasks involving test runners, mocking, and code coverage in a Node.js environment.
    - [Advanced Recipes](.roo/vendor/node/node-js-design-patterns-fourth-edition-luciano-mammino-mario-casciaro/11-advanced-recipes.md): Advanced Node.js asynchronous programming tasks, including asynchronous component initialization, high-load API request handling (batching and caching), execution of cancelable asynchronous flows, and processing of CPU-intensive (CPU-bound) computations.
    - [Scalability and Architectural Patterns](.roo/vendor/node/node-js-design-patterns-fourth-edition-luciano-mammino-mario-casciaro/12-scalability-and-architectural-patterns.md): Designing, refactoring, or implementing application scaling, load balancing, high availability, clustering, containerization (Docker/Kubernetes), microservices architecture, and service integration strategies within a Node.js ecosystem.
    - [Messaging and Integration Patterns](.roo/vendor/node/node-js-design-patterns-fourth-edition-luciano-mammino-mario-casciaro/13-messaging-and-integration-patterns.md): Designing, implementing, refactoring, or debugging distributed systems, microservices integration, or messaging architectures in Node.js, including message brokers (e.g., Redis, RabbitMQ/AMQP), peer-to-peer messaging (e.g., ZeroMQ), WebSockets for real-time communication, or the implementation of messaging patterns such as Publish/Subscribe, Task Distribution (Fan-out/Fan-in), and Asynchronous Request/Reply.
